title: How a man kills what is killing him
caption:
You call it a struggle.

God calls it an enemy.

Scripture doesn't say manage it — it says kill it. Romans 8:13. Colossians 3:5. Not reduce it, not moderate it, not make peace with it. Put it to death.

Most men lose not because the sin is too powerful, but because they're negotiating. They call porn a "weakness," laziness a "season," cowardice "introversion." Language that makes them the sympathetic case — caught in something bigger than them. But you can't kill what you've agreed to coexist with.

Four moves that actually work: name the thing your enemy instead of your struggle. Find what it promises you — relief, significance, escape — and feed that hunger with the right thing. Drag it into the light with one man who won't let you off the hook. And die to it daily — not by willpower, but by the Spirit.

"If by the Spirit you put to death the deeds of the body, you will live." — Romans 8:13

The "by the Spirit" part is what men skip. That's why they keep losing.

—

Comment "kill it" for the link to ETHOS.
